question: What is the structure of a `charmcraft.yaml` file for a Kubernetes charm? Show a complete example with all required fields.

claude_output.result:
Here's a complete `charmcraft.yaml` for a Kubernetes charm with all required fields and common optional ones:

```yaml
name: my-app-k8s
type: charm
title: My Application
summary: A one-line description of my application (max 78 chars).
description: |
  A longer description of the charm, explaining what it does,
  how it works, and any important details for users.

base: ubuntu@24.04

assumes:
  - k8s-api
  - juju >= 3.6

charm-user: non-root  # root | sudoer | non-root (Juju 3.6+)

links:
  documentation: https://discourse.charmhub.io/t/my-app-k8s
  issues: https://github.com/canonical/my-app-k8s-operator/issues
  source: https://github.com/canonical/my-app-k8s-operator

containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image
    mounts:
      - storage: data
        location: /var/lib/my-app

resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for the my-app workload
    upstream-source: ubuntu/my-app:latest

storage:
  data:
    type: filesystem
    description: Persistent data storage for my-app
    location: /var/lib/my-app
    minimum-size: 1G

config:
  options:
    log-level:
      type: string
      default: info
      description: Log level (debug, info, warning, error)
    port:
      type: int
      default: 8080
      description: Port the application listens on

actions:
  restart:
    description: Restart the my-app service.
    additionalProperties: false
  backup:
    description: Create a backup of application data.
    additionalProperties: false
    params:
      destination:
        type: string
        description: Backup destination path
    required: []

provides:
  http:
    interface: http
    optional: true

requires:
  ingress:
    interface: ingress
    limit: 1
    optional: true
  database:
    interface: postgresql_client
    limit: 1
    optional: true

peers:
  replicas:
    interface: my-app-replicas
    optional: true

charm-libs:
  - lib: traefik_k8s.ingress
    version: "2"
  - lib: data_platform_libs.data_interfaces
    version: "0"

parts:
  charm:
    charm-entrypoint: src/charm.py
    charm-requirements:
      - requirements.txt

analysis:
  ignore:
    attributes:
      - framework
```

### Key Kubernetes-specific points

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `assumes: k8s-api` | Required — declares this must deploy on a K8s cloud |
| `containers` | Defines sidecar containers; each must reference a resource or bases |
| `resources` (oci-image) | Every container in `containers` needs a matching `oci-image` resource |
| `charm-user` | Sets the UID the charm hooks run as (added in Juju 3.6) |
| `name` suffix `-k8s` | Convention for K8s-specific charms |

### Minimum viable example

If you just need the bare minimum to get started:

```yaml
name: my-app-k8s
type: charm
summary: My application charm for Kubernetes.
description: Deploys my-app on Kubernetes.
base: ubuntu@24.04

assumes:
  - k8s-api

containers:
  my-app:
    resource: my-app-image

resources:
  my-app-image:
    type: oci-image
    description: OCI image for my-app
    upstream-source: ubuntu/my-app:latest
```

Run `charmcraft init --profile=kubernetes --force` to scaffold this automatically, then expand from there.
